items.title: FA fine Chelsea for Villa bottle throwing incident
items.summary: Chelsea are fined £150,000 after an unidentified member of their staff threw a bottle towards Aston Villa's bench at the end of a Premier League match in December.

items.title: Euros abuse made me not want to leave hotel - Carter
items.summary: England defender Jess Carter says abuse of female footballers is getting worse and that she has had to step back from social media after receiving racist messages during Euro 2025.

items.title: Carrick deelt slecht nieuws over geblesseerde De Ligt: 'Gaat langzamer dan gewild'
items.summary: De rentree van Matthijs de Ligt laat iets langer op zich wachten dan aanvankelijk gedacht. De verdediger heeft te maken gehad met een kleine terugval in zijn rugblessure. Dat vertelde interim-manager Michael Carrick vrijdag op de persconferentie in aanloop naar de competitiewedstrijd tegen Arsenal.

items.title: Then and now: what has changed since the opening Premier League games?
items.summary: This weekend’s fixtures are the reverse of a first round that saw Arsenal win at Old Trafford and Sunderland start with a bang Start-of-season shape: Continue reading...

items.title: Carl Hoefkens breekt met 'heilige huisjes' bij NAC: 'Waakzaam voor oude patronen'
items.summary: Carl Hoefkens heeft het zwaar bij NAC Breda. De trainer wist al acht competitiewedstrijden niet meer te winnen, waardoor zijn ploeg op een degradatieplaats is beland. Tóch is er ook een lichtpuntje: de entree van Moussa Soumano. En dat heeft mogelijk gevolgen voor Sydney van Hooijdonk.

items.title: Twee duels in de Keuken Kampioen Divisie afgelast wegens vorst
items.summary: De wedstrijd tussen SC Cambuur en FC Eindhoven van vrijdagavond gaat niet door. Een dag later gaat er ook een streep door FC Emmen - Jong Ajax. De ploegen zouden tegenover elkaar staan in de Keuken Kampioen Divisie, maar de velden in Leeuwarden en Emmen zijn onbespeelbaar verklaard.

items.title: Sources: Arsenal exploring Álvarez summer move
items.summary: Arsenal are exploring the possibility of launching a summer move for Atletico Madrid forward Julián Álvarez, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Sources: L'pool, Robertson in talks with Spurs
items.summary: Tottenham Hotspur have entered talks with Liverpool over a potential deal for defender Andy Robertson, sources told ESPN.

items.title: UK police criticize Infantino for British fan joke
items.summary: FIFA president Gianni Infantino's joke about British fans' behaviour at the 2022 Qatar World Cup was "neither helpful nor accurate," the UK's football policing lead has said.

items.title: Arsenal hoopt op Saliba-scenario met verhuur van toptalent Nwaneri
items.summary: Arsenal heeft van de winterse transferperiode gebruikgemaakt door toptalent Ethan Nwaneri te verhuren aan Olympique Marseille . De koploper van de Premier League hoopt daarbij op het William Saliba-scenario.

items.title: Billionaire gambler Tony Bloom denies owing millions to former colleague
items.summary: Brighton & Hove Albion football club owner confirms placing bets through accounts of Reform UK adviser George Cottrell, according to legal documents The billionaire owner of Brighton & Hove Albion football club has confirmed his syndicate placed millions of pounds worth of bets through the gambling accounts of the Reform UK adviser George Cottrell. The admission comes in a document filed to the high court by Tony Bloom, who also admits that he, Cottrell and a former employee, Ryan Dudfield, had an agreement under which winnings were due to be split between them. Continue reading...

items.title: Palmer 'very, very happy' at Chelsea, says Rosenior
items.summary: Reports suggest Chelsea forward Cole Palmer is homesick amid links to Manchester United.

items.title: Premier League Power Rankings: Rating all 20 teams on performances, not results
items.summary: In the debut of ESPN's Premier League Power Rankings, a look at all 20 teams, including why Liverpool, Chelsea and Tottenham are better than the table suggests.

items.title: Keep, Dump or Extend: How far will Chelsea go to back Liam Rosenior?
items.summary: Will Chelsea add cover at center back in January to support their new coach?

items.title: Glasner '100% committed' to Palace for rest of season
items.summary: Oliver Glasner says he is "100% committed" to Crystal Palace for the remainder of his time as manager of the club.

items.title: Hamilton 'massively excited' for F1 2026 'new beginning'
items.summary: Lewis Hamilton says he is "massively excited" for "new beginnings" heading into Formula 1's latest rules era after he drove Ferrari's 2026 car for the first time.

items.title: PSV-tegenstander Bayern is een diesel: Rekordmeister komt opvallend traag op gang
items.summary: Mocht PSV er woensdag halverwege goed voorstaan tegen Bayern München, zegt dat nog niet zoveel. Der Rekordmeister is dit seizoen bij vlagen een diesel gebleken. Na een rustige eerste helft, kan het na rust zo maar eens losgaan. Dat weten ook aanvoerder Joshua Kimmich en trainer Vincent Kompany.

items.title: Borthwick: England's prop crisis is disruptive - but we'll attack Six Nations
items.summary: England head coach Steve Borthwick has conceded to Sky Sports his squad's prop injury crisis is "unfortunate and disruptive" but says they will "attack the tournament" in search of a first Six Nations title since 2020.

items.title: McIlroy seven shots back as Reed leads at halfway stage in Dubai
items.summary: Rory McIlroy is seven shots adrift of leader Patrick Reed at the halfway stage of the Hero Dubai Desert Classic as he aims to win the event for a third time in four years.

items.title: American soccer is odd, growing, and endlessly fascinating | Pablo Iglesias Maurer
items.summary: I have joined the Guardian today as a soccer correspondent in the United States. This is why I cover the sport There’s an old pennant hanging on the wall of my office here in Washington DC, tucked between a poster of indoor soccer legend Steve Zungul and a photo of Pelé riding a horse. “Soccer,” it reads, “the sport of the 80s.” For a century or so, soccer was always the sport of the next decade. Clear-thinking businesspeople tried everything to sell it to Americans, but soccer was always considered too foreign and exotic, an activity best practiced and consumed by outsiders. Even in the mid-80s, when I started playing, it was still very much othered. It’s what drew me to the sport in the first place. Pablo joins the Guardian as part of our ongoing expansion covering soccer in the United States ahead of the 2026 World Cup. He arrives alongside two other new hires: soccer correspondent Jeff Rueter and assistant sports editor Ella Brockway. He is based in Washington DC. Continue reading...
